Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Corey (trustee) in propria persona.

Mr. Corey stated that Virginia moved to Catalpa Street after he filed the petition. The court noted that the investments regarding the second and third accounting were done by the trustee without court approval. Mr. Corey expressed that he disagrees with the probate examiner's position with respect to the investments in mutual funds, and concern about the need to get court approval every time he is invests assets. Discussion was held regarding the probate code versus the local rules. The court reminds Mr. Corey that if any losses occur regarding the investments, the trustee would be liable for such losses. The court suggested that Mr. Corey file a declaration regarding his position that the trustee has not ignored any of the court orders. The Petition for Approval of Fourth Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Malone/Santos. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-13-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Corey, in propria persona, Trustee.

The court and Mr. Corey discuss the issue of making investments without court approval. Mr. Corey offers testimony regarding the court's concerns regarding the preceding. The court offers its position regarding the issue of the Trustee making new investments without prior court approval and advises that he should obtain permission to do so by petitioning the court. The court advises Mr. Corey to file fee requests contemporaneously with each accounting, moving forward.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Corey, Esq., and Administrator in pro per (appearing by video conference).

The Court advises counsel at the outset that payment of fees may only be made upon approval by the Court and finds it disconcerting that this stricture was violated by counsel. Mr. Corey offers explanation and apology for the error as he is fully aware of the approval requirement.

The Court will take the Order to Appear off calendar and the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Johnson. (D204)

Petition To Allow Payment Of Trustees Compensation; Declaration Of Joseph P. Corey
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Corey in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the Trustee's fees for the previous year were at about half of what is being requested and questions why they went up. Mr. Corey stated that it was for preparing and filing of the first accounting, that it was about 19 hours, that he had ten continuances, that there were a lot of issues regarding the investment tools and that the Beneficiary is very high maintenance. Mr. Corey also stated that the beginning of the timesheet is paralegal time, that the meetings were with the Beneficiary and mostly about money issues, things that she needs, vacation, etc., that the e-mails were to J P Morgan because he was having trouble with his password and that the e-filed documents get rejected quite a bit. In addition, Mr. Corey stated that Ruby is the lady who organized the trip to the Holy Land, that Mark is the CPA, that Josh is the Financial Planner and that Madeline is a friend the Beneficiary owed money to. The Petition to Allow Payment of Trustee's Compensation is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of First Account; Declaration Of Joseph P. Corey
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Continued To Jan-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Corey (Trustee) in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Mr. Corey that the Probate Examiner's notes need to be addressed in writing, stated that there are three pages of issues, that there are very specific things he needs to answer, and that there are accounting issues, questions about the investments, questions about his fees, etc. Mr. Corey stated that he provided information regarding the investments, that he submitted time sheets, that he is requested the 1% guideline fee because an hourly rate would far exceed what would be appropriate and that he has answered every question. The Petition for Approval of First Account is continued to January 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to respond to all of the issues raised in the Examiner's notes in writing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Corey for and with (petitioner) Virginia Cacal.

The Court stated that it has an issue with the request to waive bond and asked Ms. Cacal if she understood what that means. Ms. Cacal replied that it is like an insurance policy, that she does want to waive it, that she trusts her attorney and confirmed that she understands the consequences. Mr. Corey stated that the issue is the cost of the bond, that the assets total $390,000.00, that Ms. Cacal is only 60 years old and that this is all of her money for the remainder of her life, that her understanding of the issues is good and that they have gone over it several times. The Court granted the petition re substituted judgment (subject to review of the order) stating that the issue of bond will be under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
